originalTitle: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
content: Sixteen pupils, aged between 15 and 18, died when a fire broke out in a dormitory at Utumishi Girls' School last month.

originalTitle: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
title: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
content: The scale and brutality of the sexual attacks is unprecedented and aimed at terrorising communities, the UN says.

originalTitle: 'VAR went for a coffee' - should Ghana have had penalty against England?
title: Our Amazing Ghana Black Stars and a Mystery on the Field!
content:
Hello from Africa, Super Friends!

Hello, super friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter from Africa, here to tell you about something exciting that happened in the world of football! You know football, right? That super fun game where everyone kicks a ball and tries to score goals! Our very own heroes, the Ghana Black Stars, played a big match! Did you know football is the most popular sport in Africa? Everyone loves to watch and play!

Ghana's Black Stars Play a Big Game!

Our amazing Ghana Black Stars went to play a game against a team called England. They are very good players from far away! Everyone was so excited to watch our team play. We all cheered loudly for our Black Stars! Clap, clap, clap for Ghana! Did you know Ghana's team is called the Black Stars? It's named after a special star on our country's flag, which means hope and pride!

What is a Goal? What is a Draw?

In football, when you kick the ball into the special net, it's called a goal! That's how you get points and win. But sometimes, like in this game, no goals are scored by either team. That means the score is zero-zero. We call that a 'draw.' It means both teams are super strong, and no one won or lost the game today. Both teams get one point!

A Special Kick Called a Penalty!

Sometimes, during a game, if a player does something a little bit naughty, like pushing someone over inside a special box near the goal, the other team gets a 'penalty kick.' A penalty kick is a big chance to score a goal! One player stands all by themselves and kicks the ball towards the goal, with only the goalkeeper to stop them. It's a very exciting moment! Did you know the goalkeeper is the only player who can use their hands in the game, but only inside their special box?

The Tricky Moment in the Game

So, in our Black Stars' game against England, something tricky happened! One of our Ghana players was running super fast with the ball. And then, oops! An England player bumped into him inside that special box. Our player fell down! Many people watching, including me, thought, 'Oh no! That looks like a penalty!' Everyone held their breath!

What is VAR? Our Robot Friend with Eyes!

Now, let me tell you about a super helpful robot friend in football. It's called VAR! That stands for Video Assistant Referee. VAR is like having super special robot eyes watching the game on a big TV screen. When something tricky happens, the referee, who is the boss on the field, can ask VAR to help him look again. VAR can show slow-motion replays, just like when you watch a cool movie scene again! Did you know VAR started being used in big football games only a few years ago? It's quite new and helps make games fair!

VAR's Big Job: Helping the Referee

VAR's job is to help the referee make sure everything is fair. If the referee missed something important, or made a mistake, VAR can tell him. It's like having a helpful friend whisper in your ear, 'Hey, did you see that?' VAR can help with goals, red cards, and yes, even important penalties! It wants to make sure the right decision is made for everyone. Did you know the referee uses a whistle to start and stop the game? He is the one in charge!

Was It a Penalty for Ghana? The Big Question!

So, when our Ghana player fell down in the box, everyone thought, 'Will the referee ask VAR to check for a penalty?' It really looked like it could be a penalty! If Ghana got a penalty, it would be a big chance to score a goal and maybe even win the game for our country! But guess what? The referee did not ask VAR to check. He just said 'Play on!' Many people were confused and a little bit sad because it was such an important moment.

Why Did VAR Not Help? A Mystery!

This is the big mystery! Why didn't VAR help the referee look again at our Ghana player falling down? Was VAR busy? Did VAR not see it clearly? The original news even joked, 'VAR went for a coffee!' That means VAR might have missed a very important moment for our Black Stars. It made many Ghana fans feel a bit disappointed, because they really wanted VAR to check and make sure the game was super fair.

Why a Penalty Matters So Much!

Getting a penalty kick in football is a huge deal! It's one of the very best chances to score a goal. Imagine our Black Stars player kicking that ball right into the net! A goal can change the whole game. It can make a team win! So, missing out on a possible penalty felt very important. It could have changed the score from zero-zero to one-zero for Ghana! That's why everyone cared so much.

The End of the Game

In the end, even with all the excitement and the tricky moment with the penalty, the game finished with a zero-zero draw. No goals for England, and no goals for our Ghana Black Stars. Our players tried their very best! They ran, they kicked, they dribbled! They showed great strength and skill, and we are so proud of them for playing their hearts out for Ghana.

What We Learn from Football

Even when things are a bit confusing or don't go our way, football teaches us to be strong and keep trying. Our Black Stars are heroes, no matter what! They always play with big hearts for Ghana and for all of Africa. We are so proud of them! We learn that sometimes things don't go exactly as we hope, but we always keep our heads up, smile, and look forward to the next game with hope.

Let's Keep Cheering for Ghana!

So, let's keep cheering for our Ghana Black Stars! They are amazing, and we love watching them play. Maybe next time, VAR will be wide awake and help with all the big decisions to make sure everything is fair. Hooray for Ghana! Hooray for football! Keep playing and keep smiling, everyone!
summary: Ghana's Black Stars played a football game against England that ended in a zero-zero draw, but many fans felt Ghana should have gotten a penalty kick, and wondered why VAR, the video helper, didn't check the play.
whyMatters: This matters because a penalty kick is a big chance to score a goal and win a game, and everyone wants to make sure football games are fair for all the teams, especially for our African heroes, the Black Stars.

originalTitle: Our life stops’: West Bank childhood shattered by Israeli military raids
title: Our life stops’: West Bank childhood shattered by Israeli military raids
content: A new UN report documents killings, detentions and lasting psychological harm to Palestinian children.

originalTitle: Ivan Cepeda concedes defeat in Colombia election, sealing right-wing win
title: Ivan Cepeda concedes defeat in Colombia election, sealing right-wing win
content: Presidential candidate Ivan Cepeda accepted the victory of his opponent Abelardo de la Espriella.

originalTitle: Palestinian boy amongst dead in Israeli strike on displaced tent in Gaza
title: Palestinian boy amongst dead in Israeli strike on displaced tent in Gaza
content: An Israeli drone strike on a tent sheltering displaced Palestinians in Khan Younis killed two people, including a child.

originalTitle: Power outages hit France as it records hottest day since measurements began
title: Power outages hit France as it records hottest day since measurements began
content: France has recorded it hottest day since records began in 1947, its national weather agency says, breaking a record set on Tuesday.

originalTitle: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
title: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
content: France is being forced to re-think its longstanding reservations about air-conditioning as temperatures soar.

originalTitle: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
content: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

originalTitle: Scotland primed for game of their lives against fallible Brazil
title: Scotland primed for game of their lives against fallible Brazil
content: Scotland don't know what they need against Brazil to reach the World Cup knockout phase, but will know they need to improve on the previous two games, writes Tom English.

originalTitle: England's James and Rice to have fitness assessed
title: England's James and Rice to have fitness assessed
content: England will assess the fitness of Reece James and Declan Rice before their final World Cup group game against Panama.

title: England to face Scotland? World Cup knockout stage as it stands
